name: differential-test
description: Create a differential C++ ↔ Rust test for a migrated component. Use when a new crate reaches feature parity with its C++ counterpart.
steps: 1. Identify the C++ function signature you're mirroring in Rust
2. Add `extern "C"` export in Rust for the comparison entry point
3. Add `extern "C"` import in C++ test (or expose via capi.h)
4. Create test inputs (IR programs, x86 bytes, config) that exercise both paths
8. Add to CI workflow ffi-link so it runs on every PR

template.rust_export: #[no_mangle]\npub extern "C" fn prisma_rust_validate(stmts: *const Stmt, len: u32) -> bool { ... }
template.cpp_caller: extern "C" bool prisma_rust_validate(const ir::Stmt* stmts, uint32_t len);
